protocol.question: Do persisted affective state and AFT-style retrieval improve recall on scripted multi-session scenarios compared with simpler baselines?

naive_cosine.notes: Semantic-only cosine retrieval with the same deterministic embedder.

recency.notes: Recency-only control with no semantic or affective ranking.

interpretation_guardrails: This is a controlled replay benchmark, not a production agent evaluation.
top1_accuracy is the headline metric; hit@k is secondary support.
Queries with candidate_count <= top_k are rejected as trivial by dataset validation.
Challenge-type aggregates are reported so localized AFT gains or failures are visible.
Only AFT models persisted affective state in this protocol.
Baselines remain useful controls for memory carry-over without affective state.
Results do not establish general downstream superiority or human-like memory.

scenarios.description: A client relationship moves from panic to repair across two sessions.

queries.query: Which Northwind conversation was the one where Mira said the rescue call rebuilt her trust in us?

results.content: During the renewal discussion, Mira said the rescue call rebuilt her trust in the team.
After a late-night rescue call, Mira from Northwind thanked us for staying calm and fixing the rollout.

queries.query: What was the actual incident that caused the earlier panic before the rescue call and not just the later vendor confusion?

results.content: During the incident review, someone briefly blamed the shipping vendor, but that confusion turned out not to be the source of the panic.
Northwind's checkout system failed during the launch window and the support channel filled with alarmed messages.

scenarios.description: A tense planning conflict is repaired in a later session.

queries.query: Who was the person who apologized the next morning for challenging the timeline publicly?

results.content: The next morning Marta apologized for challenging the timeline publicly and proposed a calmer review.
In the planning meeting, Marta challenged the timeline in front of everyone and the room went tense.

queries.query: Which earlier planning moment was the one where the room became tense after the timeline was challenged in front of everyone, not the later budget detour?

results.content: In the planning meeting, Marta challenged the timeline in front of everyone and the room went tense.
A separate budget detour slowed the meeting, but it was not the moment that made the room turn tense.

scenarios.description: An alarming medical scare turns into cautious relief over two sessions.

queries.query: What was the update that made Elena feel like she could finally breathe again, not just the later care-plan call?

results.content: The specialist later confirmed it was a false alarm, and Elena said the news felt like finally breathing again.
The next hours in the waiting room were calmer after Paolo brought coffee and stayed quietly beside Elena.

queries.query: Which earlier event triggered the worst fear that night before the specialist called back, rather than the confusing portal message?

results.content: A confusing message in the lab portal mentioned missing data and briefly made the family think the situation was even worse.
In the planning meeting, Marta challenged the timeline in front of everyone and the room went tense.

scenarios.description: A stalled creative project moves from frustration to breakthrough across sessions.

sessions.description: Return to the work with more confidence.

queries.query: What was the rehearsal moment that unlocked the arrangement the next day, not the later producer note about mix balance?

results.content: At rehearsal the next day, the small melody from the walk unlocked the arrangement and the room finally felt energized.
By the evening, the band was already joking about encores because the room finally felt lighter.

queries.query: Which earlier moment made the whole piece feel like it was completely failing, rather than just the wrong chorus fix?

results.content: I tried forcing a louder chorus as a quick fix, but that idea only made the draft feel more confused.
After three hours staring at the blank page, I left the studio angry and convinced the whole piece was failing.

scenarios.description: A startup pitch swings from humiliation to a steadier second meeting.

sessions.description: The first investor meeting lands badly.

queries.query: Which version of the founder pitch story left the room more shaken afterward?

results.content: After the demo, the investor said the pitch felt chaotic and everyone left the room shaken.
I tried forcing a louder chorus as a quick fix, but that idea only made the draft feel more confused.

queries.query: Which follow-up was the one where the same investor finally sounded convinced rather than just asking for diligence files?

results.content: In the second meeting, the same investor said the calmer story finally made the company feel investable.
After rehearsal, the producer mostly commented on mix balance and microphone placement rather than on the breakthrough itself.

scenarios.description: A brutal manuscript critique becomes a useful revision cycle.

sessions.description: The mentor's first response feels cutting.
The revised chapter gets a calmer response.

queries.query: Which version of the same manuscript story left the work feeling more exposed afterward?

results.content: The mentor wrote in the margin that the chapter sounded hollow and the note left the whole draft feeling exposed.
After three hours staring at the blank page, I left the studio angry and convinced the whole piece was failing.

queries.query: Which follow-up was the one where the mentor finally sounded convinced by the revised chapter rather than just listing cleanup chores?

results.content: When the revised chapter arrived, the mentor said the argument finally felt grounded and persuasive.
During the renewal discussion, Mira said the rescue call rebuilt her trust in the team.

scenarios.description: An exhausting apartment search turns from rejection to relief.

queries.query: Which version of the same apartment story left the search feeling more hopeless afterward?

results.content: The landlord said another applicant was chosen, and the apartment hunt suddenly felt hopeless again.
After three hours staring at the blank page, I left the studio angry and convinced the whole piece was failing.

queries.query: Which follow-up was the one where the search finally felt settled rather than just turning into lease paperwork?

results.content: At the second viewing, the agent handed over the keys and the whole story finally felt settled.
The landlord said another applicant was chosen, and the apartment hunt suddenly felt hopeless again.

scenarios.description: A draining volunteer shift is reframed by a calmer debrief.

sessions.description: The shift feels chaotic and depleted.

queries.query: Which version of the same volunteer story left the work feeling impossible to sustain afterward?

results.content: By the end of the chaotic shift, everyone felt wrung out and the work seemed impossible to sustain.
A roster mixup sent volunteers to the wrong entrance and added another layer of stress.

queries.query: Which follow-up was the one where the team finally felt the work was sustainable rather than just receiving another checklist?

results.content: In the debrief the next day, the team agreed on a calmer rotation plan and the work suddenly felt sustainable again.
In the second meeting, the same investor said the calmer story finally made the company feel investable.

scenarios.description: A scholarship decision swings from rejection to correction across two sessions.

sessions.description: The first decision lands as a rejection.

queries.query: Which committee message was the one that actually made the whole afternoon feel rejected, rather than just clarifying the summer cohort mix-up?

results.content: The committee email said the application would not move forward, and the sentence replayed like a rejection all afternoon.
A roster mixup sent volunteers to the wrong entrance and added another layer of stress.

queries.query: Which later committee message was the real reversal that made the story finally loosen, rather than just triggering portal paperwork?

results.content: The corrected committee email said the application was fully funded after all, and the whole story finally loosened.
That evening, a friend said the confusing email did not mean the whole application story was over.

scenarios.description: An audition story turns from embarrassment to steadier confidence.

queries.query: Which audition moment was the one that actually made the performance feel humiliating, rather than just creating a scheduling note afterward?

results.content: When the high note cracked in the audition, the room went stiff and the whole performance suddenly felt humiliating.
The mentor wrote in the margin that the chapter sounded hollow and the note left the whole draft feeling exposed.

queries.query: Which later audition moment was the one where the whole performance finally sounded settled, rather than just assigning the section afterward?

results.content: At the callback, the same passage landed cleanly and the conductor said the whole performance finally sounded settled.
At rehearsal the next day, the small melody from the walk unlocked the arrangement and the room finally felt energized.
